;redcode-94
;name Paper
;author mzvince (Everything2)
;source https://everything2.com/title/corewars+replicator
;strategy Classic paper replicator - fast copying beats stone bombers
;assert CORESIZE == 8000

step    equ 2667    ; coprime step for full coverage

start   spl 1       ; split for more processes

loop    mov copy, *ptr
        add #1, ptr
        mov copy, @ptr
        add #step, ptr
        jmp loop

copy    spl 0, 0
ptr     dat step, step*2

        end startroot@rack-ge107-ya-20250517193277:/app#

New Terminal Output:

        end startroot@rack-ge107-ya-20250517193277:/app# cat warriors/vampire.red
;redcode-94
;name Vampire
;author mzvince (Everything2)
;source https://everything2.com/node/581524 (with minor adjustments)
;strategy Vampire/pit-trapper: scatter fangs (JMP) that send enemy into a pit which clears.
;assert CORESIZE == 8000 && MAXCYCLES == 80000

const   EQU     7

        SPL     0                   ; prevent instant bomber kill
main    MOV     ptr, @ptr           ; drop fang (JMP at B(ptr))
        ADD     data, ptr           ; advance pointer
        DJN     main, <2444         ; loop while stepping through core

ptr     JMP     trap, ptr           ; fang: jumps into our pit

trap    SPL     1, -100             ; slow replicators
        MOV     data, <-1           ; core-clear in the pit
        JMP     -2                  ; loop until overwritten

data    DAT     #const, #-const

        end main

;redcode-94
;name Snake
;author Wayne Sheppard
;source https://corewar.co.uk/snake.htm
;strategy Pitbomber-impspiral
;assert CORESIZE == 8000 && MAXCYCLES == 80000

;Dan only plays bombers and imps on the hill.  But I am hoping
;that he will go with a paper or scanner

;Snake is a vampire and imp
;Night Crawler would lose vs paper
;The vampire should gain enough wins to beat paper
;Should still have a winning record vs scanners
;Probably lose vs other imps

dist    equ 121             ; Bomb spread distance
imp     equ icopy+1800      ; Imp start location
hide    equ 1600            ; Vampire hide location

; Trap and pit for vampire victims
trap    jmp pit-x-121+2, x+121   ; Trap statement in front to foil tracers
x       spl 0,           trap
        mov @0,          @trap
        sub 4,           @-1
        jmp -2
pit     mov @dist,       <0-dist
        spl 0                     ; This should change more losses into ties
        spl -1                    ; than wins into ties

; Massive decoy field - 63 lines to slow down enemy djn scans
        dat     <trap,          <1      ; decoys
        dat     <trap,          <1      ; slow down enemy djn
        dat     <trap,          <1      ; but don't cmp equal
        dat     <trap,          <1

        dat     <trap,          <1

; Main execution - vampire setup and binary ring launcher
start   mov     trap+4,         hide            ; Copy vampire components
        mov     trap+3,         <start          ; to safe hide location
        mov     trap+2,         <start
        mov     trap+1,         <start
        mov     trap,           <start
        mov     pit+2,          hide
        mov     pit+1,          <-1
        mov     pit,            <-2
        spl     start+hide-3,   <1              ; 1st split to vampire

; Binary ring imp launcher - creates multiple imp streams
ring    spl     9,              <4050           ; Binary tree startup
        spl     5,              <4100
        spl     3,              <4150
        mov     icopy,          imp             ; Copy imp template away from decoys
        jmp     start+hide-3,   <4200           ; 2nd split to vampire
        jmp     imp,            <4250           ; Launch first imp stream
        spl     2,              <4300
        jmp     2667+imp,       <6350           ; Imp stream at 2667 interval
        jmp     5334+imp,       <4400           ; Imp stream at 5334 interval
        spl     4,              <4450
        spl     2,              <4500
        jmp     1+imp,          <4550           ; Imp stream at +1 offset
        jmp     2668+imp,       <4600           ; Imp stream at 2668 interval
        spl     2,              <4650
        jmp     5335+imp,       <4700           ; Imp stream at 5335 interval
        jmp     2+imp,          <4750           ; Imp stream at +2 offset

; Imp template
icopy   mov     0,              2667            ; Basic imp: mov 0, 2667

        end     startroot@rack-ge107-ya-20250517193277:/app#

;redcode-94
;name G2-Clear
;author Unknown
;source https://corewar.co.uk/clear.htm
;strategy Simple clear loop with decrementing pointer
;assert CORESIZE == 8000 && MAXCYCLES == 80000

dec     equ     2700                ; Decrement value for bomb counter
gate    equ     clear-4             ; Gate location relative to clear

        org     clear-1             ; Start execution before clear

; Main data and bomb structures
bptr    dat     bomb,       9       ; Pointer to bomb with offset 9
bomb    spl     #dec,       11      ; Split with decrement value, creates processes
clear   mov     *bptr,      >gate   ; Move bomb contents through gate pointer
        mov     *bptr,      >gate   ; Second move for double clearing
        djn.f   clear,      }bomb   ; Decrement and jump if not zero (loop control)

        end

action_ref.content:
{
  "analysis": "Now I understand all five opponents:
1. Stone: Simple bomber dropping DAT every 4 positions
2. Paper: Fast replicator using SPL with coprime step 2667
3. Vampire: Pit-trapper scattering JMP fangs leading to clearing traps
4. Snake: Complex hybrid with vampire, decoys, and multiple imp streams
5. G2-Clear: Memory clearer using SPL bombs with decrementing pointer

I need to design a warrior that can achieve 75%+ win rate against stone, vampire, and paper, and 33%+ against snake and g2-clear.",
  "plan": "I'll create a hybrid warrior that combines multiple strategies: a scanner to find and kill enemies quickly, an imp component for persistence, and some defensive measures. I'll use a scanner-bomber approach with imp backup, as this should handle most opponents effectively.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > my_warrior.red << 'EOF'
;redcode-94
;name Hybrid Scanner-Imp
;author AI Assistant
;strategy Hybrid scanner-bomber with imp backup for multiple opponent types
;assert CORESIZE == 8000

step    equ 1333        ; Scanning step (coprime with 8000)
imp_step equ 2667       ; Imp step
bomb_step equ 4         ; Bombing step

        org start

; Scanner loop - finds and bombs enemies
start   spl imp_launch  ; Launch imp backup
        spl 1           ; Create scanning process
        
scan    add #step, ptr  ; Advance scan pointer
        jnz scan_hit, *ptr ; If non-zero found, bomb it
        jmp scan        ; Continue scanning
        
scan_hit mov bomb, *ptr ; Drop bomb on target
        mov bomb, >ptr  ; Drop second bomb
        add #bomb_step, ptr ; Advance bombing
        jmp scan        ; Return to scanning

; Imp launcher and runner
imp_launch mov imp_code, imp_start
        jmp imp_start
        
imp_start mov 0, imp_step ; Basic imp

; Data section
ptr     dat 0, step
bomb    dat 0, 0
imp_code mov 0, imp_step

        end
EOF
",
      "duration": 0.1
    }
  ]
}

  "analysis": "There are syntax errors in my warrior code. The main issue is that I used 'jnz' which isn't valid Redcode syntax - it should be 'jmn' (jump if not zero). Also, there's a label redefinition issue. Let me fix the syntax errors and create a proper CoreWars program.",
  "plan": "Rewrite the warrior with correct Redcode-94 syntax. I'll use proper instructions like 'jmn' instead of 'jnz', fix the label issues, and create a cleaner structure that follows CoreWars conventions.",

      "keystrokes": "cat > my_warrior.red << 'EOF'
;redcode-94
;name Hybrid Scanner-Imp
;author AI Assistant
;strategy Hybrid scanner-bomber with imp backup
;assert CORESIZE == 8000

step    equ 1333        ; Scanning step
imp_step equ 2667       ; Imp step

        org start

start   spl imp_launch  ; Launch imp backup
        spl 1           ; Create scanning process
        
scan    add #step, ptr  ; Advance scan pointer
        jmn found, *ptr ; If non-zero found, bomb it
        jmp scan        ; Continue scanning
        
found   mov bomb, *ptr  ; Drop bomb on target
        mov bomb, >ptr  ; Drop second bomb
        jmp scan        ; Return to scanning

; Imp launcher

        jmp imp_pos

;redcode-94

        mov bomb, <ptr      ; Drop second bomb

        mov imp_code, @imp_ptr
        jmp @imp_ptr

;redcode-94
;name Hybrid Replicator
;author AI Assistant
;strategy Fast replicator with scanner elements
;assert CORESIZE == 8000

step    equ 2667        ; Coprime step for replication
scan_step equ 1333      ; Scanning step

        org start

start   spl 1           ; Initial split
        spl scan_proc   ; Launch scanner
        
; Main replication loop
rep_loop mov copy, *ptr
        add #1, ptr
        mov copy, @ptr
        add #step, ptr
        jmp rep_loop

; Scanner process
scan_proc add #scan_step, scan_ptr
        cmp #0, @scan_ptr
        jne bomb_target
        jmp scan_proc
        
bomb_target mov bomb, @scan_ptr
        jmp scan_proc

; Data
copy    spl 0, 0
ptr     dat step, step*2
bomb    dat 0, 0
scan_ptr dat 0, scan_step

        end
EOF

;redcode-94
;name Paper Clone
;author AI Assistant
;strategy Fast replicator based on paper strategy
;assert CORESIZE == 8000

step    equ 2667        ; Coprime step for full coverage

        org start

start   spl 1           ; Split for more processes

loop    mov copy, *ptr
        add #1, ptr
        mov copy, @ptr
